inline_comments.message: do you need to catch exception for invalid integer?
new to throw an exxeption for anything below -1 (ie. customer can pass -2)
suggestion: extractto this in a separate (dedicated) utility class so it can be tested and covered fro all ecnatio in a very basic unit test
total duplication so another good reason to extract this out!

as I mentioned before, should not be tested here bu in Spec for dedicated conversion class
and when you have that aslo test
-2 and 'invalid-value'  expectiong a certain type of exception

not sure if this need to be tested here
(there is a maintenance cost with too many tests!)
once the conversion is sorted in a separate class, I dont hink these tests add value
re used at the test above so can maybe extract out ?
also re used on test below and above so extract out
does this need the api version?
I think this might be better off in the FetchDescendantOption Class, we might also need error handling for it? Maybe make an InvalidDescendantsOptionException
same comment, in FetchDescendantOption class would remove duplicate code
I think custom exception is necessary here
These scenarios don't seem to be testing anything as output is always the same? descendants in the expected results column but is used as an input only
yes we need to version this API because we are replacing existing parameter includeDescendantsOptionInQuery with descendants.
Not needed because v1 is hardcoded and deprecated
not required because v1 and v2 are hardcoded
we are handling allowed values of descendants in yml file using regular expression. that's why custom exception is not added. will discuss and create if required.
test cases are mainly to validating descendants for the request as it was done for above test case.
Ah I understand, will get team input to come to decision

Yeah but in the test above the expectedCpsDataServiceOption is being validated against the mock input given on line 75, something similar should be done on line 99 instead of _ in this test to achieve the same test result. The different cases are not being validated in this test
these lines are a duplicate of lines 77-87 and should be extracted out in a common private method

Exception scenarios should be tested only once for one negative use-case in org.onap.cps.rest.exceptions.CpsRestExceptionHandlerSpec

All other negative scenario should covered in the new FetchDescendantsOption  (separate commit)
descendants is an INPUT parameter not an (expeted) output so use single | instead of ||

we won't fix it now but you should have written a UNIT test for this class. then you would NOT have to repeat tests for all the scenarios in the 2 controller tests....

Maybe do this as a separate commit before closing the user story!

no a great name.
Maybe 'executeNodesByDataspaceQuery'
or 'getDataNodesAsListOfMaps'

If you include the response create I would rename this method to
executeNodesByDataspaceQueryAndCreateResponse


(nothing wrong with long descriptive method names)
should not need scenarios here, just test with one of the positive scenarios e.g. 'all'
should not need scenarios here, just test with one of the positive scenarios e.g. 'all'

be more specific, the kind of exception is important and you are asserting for it too, imagine the reader of these descriptions cannot understand/see the java code (this actually does happen when the testresult are processed by certain tool, it will only show descriptions like this) :

a data validation exception is thrown

ah, I was wondering about that, didn't spot it in the production code that the exception was thrown
and as ewe already have controller exeception test for this exception you didnt need to add any test for that :-)
great, no you have tested this here you dont need to repeat those variation in the controller tests as I previously commented
removed this test from here to CpsRestExceptionHandlerSpec.groovy
I discussed with Luke and removing this validation check because it is redundant

The exception tested in controller is different. in controller we are getting Bad request 400 error because descendant parameter in configured in components.yml to accept only all | none | -1 | 0 to n . we added this validation for extra check but not used

we cannot validate in REST layer as the validation is/should be determined by the service layer. (now you are duplicating this)
(and added advantage is that the error handling is already covered as long as you re-use existing exceptions)

See the pattern in all the other tests: the controller/method details of the use case are NOT important for the exception handler (ie. this test claas) 

all that happens is your throw an validation exception and that's converted to a BAD_REQUEST 


ie. you do NOT need to add any new test, that is the beauty of good unit tests!

restore check validation from path #14
but extract out the compiled pattern as a constant not the regex string
removed this validation since it is duplicate. added this validation in service
ok since data validation exception is already covered so removing these redundant test cases

sorry I probably didn't focus on the naming before because of teh functional issue but I think now functionality is OK this should be changed.
'descendants' are the objects targeted by this option so lets not mix those names up but be precise

the correct name here is fetchDescendantsOptionAsString  (because you are converting it)

NB: I prefer Strings.isNullOrEmpty() for readability
(maybe next time)
Also, is this check really necessary does the matcher not handle nul/empty ?!
optional (depends on your perference): sometime for test it is clear to use the term 'result'
changes accordingly. checking of null using regular expression does not work
NB: the point of using def was you dont have to specify a  class, I should have been clearer about that
